mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase borrowing costs and compress equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean during rate hikes with p < 0.05, or Rank IC remains below 0.03 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances when UNRATE rises > 0.2pp, or trigger fails to separate return distributions at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and builder confidence, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST yields negative 60-day SPX returns in > 55% of cases, or interaction term loses statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth signals strengthening corporate output capacity and revenue momentum, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days during economic expansions.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO growth correlates with negative 20-day SPX returns during expansion phases with a rank IC below -0.02.

mechanism_narrative: High policy rates combined with rising unemployment indicate monetary policy is overtightening relative to labor market slack, forcing equity repricing and generating negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition of FEDFUNDS > 4.5% and rising UNRATE fails to predict negative 20-day returns with statistical significance (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation reading exceeding 0.4% triggers immediate market expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, causing sharp negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL spikes above 0.4% monthly are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns in > 60% of observations, or signal loses predictive power after 2005.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects robust economic activity and corporate hiring, supporting earnings growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive PAYEMS growth consistently coincides with negative 60-day SPX returns across multiple market cycles, or Rank IC < 0.01 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production alongside slowing GDP growth signals a contraction in aggregate demand and supply chain bottlenecks, driving negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days during late-cycle phases.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO slope combined with slowing GDPC1 yields positive 20-day SPX returns in > 58% of late-cycle observations, or trigger shows no out-of-sample predictive accuracy.
